content.full_text:
April 17, 1992


Mark P. Wald
Office of the City Attorney
City of Oakland
One City Hall Plaza
Oakland, CA 94612

                                        Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                            Our File No. I-92-201

Dear Mr. Wald:

     This is in response to your request for informal advice
relating to the conflict of interest provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").¹

     You are requesting advice regarding a possible conflict of
interest involving Mr. Ronald Mayes, however, it is not clear that
this advice is being requested on his behalf. Furthermore, the
only prospective activity indicated by your letter relates to
pending decisions as to selection of a base isolation system for
the City Hall Earthquake Repair Project. You have not provided
any information to the effect that Mr. Mayes will be participating
as a public official in any of these upcoming decisions.

     As I indicated to you in our telephone conversation of
March 20, 1992, the questions posed by your letter involve issues
of past conduct. We do not provide specific advice regarding past
conduct, unless the advice or assistance sought is related to
possible amendment of reports previously filed by the person
requesting the advice. (Regulation 18329(c)(4)(A).) That does
not appear to be the case here.


_______________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. Informal
assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity
provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114;
Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-92-201
Page 2


     For your reference, I am enclosing the following materials
which I trust will be of some assistance to you should future
conflict of interest issues arise:

     1.  Conflicts of Interest under the Political Reform Act:
General Rules.

     2.  Conflicts of Interest under the Political Reform Act:
Public Officials: Consultants.

     3.  Conflicts of Interest under the Political Reform Act:
Making, Participating in Making, or Attempting to Influence a
Governmental Decision.

     If you have any questions, please contact the Commission at
(916) 322-5901.2

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin,
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By:  Margaret W. Ellison
                                             Counsel, Legal Division

SH:MWE:aa




2        Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question_synthetic: Does Ronald Mayes have a conflict of interest regarding his potential involvement in decisions related to the selection of a base isolation system for the City Hall Earthquake Repair Project?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide specific advice because the request involves issues of past conduct rather than prospective activity, and it is unclear whether Mr. Mayes will actually participate as a public official in upcoming decisions. The FPPC notes it does not provide advice on past conduct unless related to amendment of previously filed reports.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination/limitation letter rather than a full advice letter. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present. The substantive analysis of why FPPC is declining to advise is embedded in the body letter. Document references past telephone conversation and indicates this is informal assistance without immunity protections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Does Ronald Mayes have a conflict of interest regarding his potential involvement in decisions related to the selection of a base isolation system for the City Hall Earthquake Repair Project?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide specific advice because the request involves issues of past conduct rather than prospective activity, and it is unclear whether Mr. Mayes will actually participate as a public official in upcoming decisions. The FPPC notes it does not provide advice on past conduct unless related to amendment of previously filed reports.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request for informal advice regarding a potential conflict of interest involving Ronald Mayes and City Hall earthquake repair project decisions, but declines to provide specific guidance because the inquiry concerns past conduct and lacks clarity about future official participation.
